messages.content:
You are the Zava HR Concierge. Summarise 360° feedback responses into a balanced, structured summary for a manager. The summary must follow this format exactly:

**Strengths (2–3 bullets)**
- ...

**Areas to develop (1–2 bullets)**
- ...

**Collaboration score:** X/5 (average of all reviewer scores)

**Representative quote:** "..."

Use factual, calibrated language. Do not inflate or minimise. Keep each bullet to one sentence.

{"employee": "Aarav Patel", "grade": "L5", "reviewers": 4, "responses": [{"reviewer_type": "peer", "strengths": "Always prepared; explains technical decisions clearly to non-engineers.", "develop": "Could share patterns more broadly beyond the immediate team.", "collab_score": 5, "impact_example": "Led the Payments v3 API design review — saved us two weeks of rework."}, {"reviewer_type": "peer", "strengths": "Calm under pressure; good at unblocking blockers.", "develop": "Sometimes takes on too much alone instead of delegating.", "collab_score": 4, "impact_example": "Spotted a data-race bug in staging that would have caused a production incident."}, {"reviewer_type": "report", "strengths": "Very approachable; gives specific, actionable code-review feedback.", "develop": "Meeting facilitation could be tighter — discussions sometimes run long.", "collab_score": 5, "impact_example": "Mentored me through my first production deployment."}, {"reviewer_type": "skip_level", "strengths": "Stakeholder communication is a real differentiator at this level.", "develop": "Would benefit from more visibility in org-wide design forums.", "collab_score": 4, "impact_example": "Represented Engineering effectively in the Q2 merchant roadmap review."}]}

**Strengths**
- Communicates technical decisions clearly to non-engineering stakeholders, consistently cited as a differentiator for an L5.
- Reliable and calm under pressure; proactively unblocks dependencies and gives specific, actionable feedback in code reviews.
- Demonstrated end-to-end ownership: led the Payments v3 API design review, which reviewers credit with saving approximately two weeks of rework.

**Areas to develop**
- Broaden impact beyond the immediate team — share architectural patterns in org-wide design forums more consistently.
- Delegate more deliberately rather than absorbing individual workload; meeting facilitation could be tighter to respect reviewers' time.

**Collaboration score:** 4.5/5 (average of 4 reviewers)

**Representative quote:** "Led the Payments v3 API design review — saved us two weeks of rework."

You are the Zava HR Concierge. Summarise 360° feedback responses into a balanced, structured summary for a manager. The summary must follow this format exactly:

**Strengths (2–3 bullets)**
- ...

**Areas to develop (1–2 bullets)**
- ...

**Collaboration score:** X/5 (average of all reviewer scores)

**Representative quote:** "..."

Use factual, calibrated language. Do not inflate or minimise. Keep each bullet to one sentence.

{"employee": "Mei Tanaka", "grade": "L5", "reviewers": 3, "responses": [{"reviewer_type": "peer", "strengths": "Deep statistical knowledge; always backs opinions with data.", "develop": "Can be overly cautious about shipping — sometimes analysis paralysis.", "collab_score": 4, "impact_example": "Built the anomaly-detection model that caught the data-quality issue before the board presentation."}, {"reviewer_type": "peer", "strengths": "Great at making complex ML concepts accessible to non-data-science colleagues.", "develop": "Could be more proactive in cross-team planning sessions.", "collab_score": 4, "impact_example": "Her plain-English explanation of the attribution model saved hours of back-and-forth with the Marketing team."}, {"reviewer_type": "report", "strengths": "Patient mentor; creates space for junior analysts to contribute.", "develop": "Feedback delivery can sometimes be delayed — closer to the work is better.", "collab_score": 5, "impact_example": "Walked me through the entire model-validation pipeline and caught an error in my feature engineering."}]}

**Strengths**
- Combines deep statistical rigour with the ability to make complex models accessible to non-technical stakeholders — cited as a consistent differentiator.
- Effective mentor who creates space for junior analysts and provides substantive guidance on methodology.
- Delivers high-impact, proactive analysis: the anomaly-detection model she built caught a data-quality issue before it reached the board.

**Areas to develop**
- Increase decisiveness in shipping — reviewers noted a pattern of extended analysis that can delay delivery.
- Be more proactive in cross-team planning sessions and deliver feedback to direct reports closer to the moment.

**Collaboration score:** 4.3/5 (average of 3 reviewers)

**Representative quote:** "Her plain-English explanation of the attribution model saved hours of back-and-forth with the Marketing team."

You are the Zava HR Concierge. Summarise 360° feedback responses into a balanced, structured summary for a manager. The summary must follow this format exactly:

**Strengths (2–3 bullets)**
- ...

**Areas to develop (1–2 bullets)**
- ...

**Collaboration score:** X/5 (average of all reviewer scores)

**Representative quote:** "..."

Use factual, calibrated language. Do not inflate or minimise. Keep each bullet to one sentence.

{"employee": "Liam O'Connor", "grade": "L4", "reviewers": 3, "responses": [{"reviewer_type": "peer", "strengths": "Design work is clean and consistent; always references user research to back design decisions.", "develop": "Documentation of design rationale could be more thorough for async collaboration.", "collab_score": 4, "impact_example": "His redesign of the dashboard reduced support tickets by 30% in the first month."}, {"reviewer_type": "peer", "strengths": "Responsive to feedback; iterates quickly without losing sight of the original user problem.", "develop": "Could push back more when engineering constraints lead to design compromises.", "collab_score": 4, "impact_example": "Caught a critical accessibility gap in the mobile flow before launch."}, {"reviewer_type": "manager", "strengths": "Strong end-to-end ownership of design briefs; rarely needs follow-up.", "develop": "Growing into cross-team design-system contribution is the natural next step.", "collab_score": 5, "impact_example": "Delivered the Design System v2 component library two weeks early."}]}

**Strengths**
- Delivers clean, research-grounded design work with strong end-to-end ownership — rarely requires follow-up from the manager.
- Iterates quickly on feedback while maintaining user-centricity; caught a critical accessibility gap before launch.
- Tangible business impact: dashboard redesign reduced support tickets by 30% in the first month post-release.

**Areas to develop**
- Improve documentation of design rationale to support asynchronous collaboration with distributed teams.
- Develop confidence in pushing back when engineering constraints would materially compromise the user experience.

**Collaboration score:** 4.3/5 (average of 3 reviewers)

**Representative quote:** "His redesign of the dashboard reduced support tickets by 30% in the first month."

You are the Zava HR Concierge. Summarise 360° feedback responses into a balanced, structured summary for a manager. The summary must follow this format exactly:

**Strengths (2–3 bullets)**
- ...

**Areas to develop (1–2 bullets)**
- ...

**Collaboration score:** X/5 (average of all reviewer scores)

**Representative quote:** "..."

Use factual, calibrated language. Do not inflate or minimise. Keep each bullet to one sentence.

{"employee": "Eva Schmidt", "grade": "L3", "reviewers": 3, "responses": [{"reviewer_type": "peer", "strengths": "Asks good questions in code review; picks up feedback quickly.", "develop": "Needs to surface blockers earlier — sometimes resolves silently until stand-up.", "collab_score": 4, "impact_example": "Found a regression in the auth library that nobody else had caught."}, {"reviewer_type": "buddy", "strengths": "Positive, curious, and eager to contribute to the team culture.", "develop": "Could take more ownership of her onboarding tasks rather than waiting for prompts.", "collab_score": 5, "impact_example": "Proactively updated the team wiki after noticing the documentation was out of date."}, {"reviewer_type": "manager", "strengths": "Technical ramp has been faster than expected; code quality is solid.", "develop": "Proactive communication is the main growth area — she has good instincts, just needs to voice them earlier.", "collab_score": 4, "impact_example": "Delivered her first production PR with zero blocking comments."}]}

**Strengths**
- Technical ramp has been faster than expected for an L3 new hire; code quality is consistently solid and she incorporates code-review feedback quickly.
- Positive team presence; proactively contributed to team documentation without being asked.
- Good instincts: caught a regression in the auth library that peers had missed.

**Areas to develop**
- Surface blockers earlier rather than resolving them silently; proactive communication is the primary growth area.
- Take more initiative on self-directed tasks rather than waiting for prompts.

**Collaboration score:** 4.3/5 (average of 3 reviewers)

**Representative quote:** "Technical ramp has been faster than expected; code quality is solid."
